description: MCP Server, Serve Command & Core Tools - Expose Seshat as an MCP server so AI agents can query project context, conventions, and record decisions

userStories.title: Add rmcp + tokio workspace dependencies and extend ServerConfig
userStories.description: As a developer, I need rmcp and tokio added to the workspace and ServerConfig extended with host/port/transports fields, so that the MCP server can be built on these foundations.
acceptanceCriteria: rmcp added as workspace dependency in root Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]
tokio added as workspace dependency with rt-multi-thread and macros features
seshat-mcp/Cargo.toml references rmcp and tokio via workspace = true
ServerConfig in crates/seshat-core/src/config.rs extended with host: String (default '127.0.0.1'), port: u16 (default 39271), transports: Vec<String> (default ['stdio', 'sse', 'http'])
seshat.example.toml updated with [server] section documenting host, port, transports options with default values and comments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundational dependency story. rmcp version should be pinned after verifying API compatibility. Header in seshat.example.toml should state: 'All values shown below are the defaults.'

userStories.title: Implement McpServer struct with rmcp stdio transport and dummy tool
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a minimal MCP server PoC using rmcp to validate API compatibility before building the full tool suite.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/seshat-mcp/src/lib.rs implements McpServer struct with async fn start() method
McpServer registers one dummy 'ping' tool via rmcp that returns a simple JSON response
Server starts with stdio transport using rmcp's transport API
McpServer accepts ServerConfig parameter for configuration
Tracing structured logging for server start/stop events
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the rmcp spike from the PRD. Validates rmcp API compatibility. The dummy tool will be replaced by real tools in later stories.

userStories.title: Implement seshat serve CLI command with DB discovery and startup/shutdown UX
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run 'seshat serve' to start the MCP server with proper DB discovery and startup/shutdown output.
acceptanceCriteria: Command::Serve in seshat-cli/src/args.rs accepts optional --host, --port flags
seshat-cli dispatch handles Command::Serve — replaces current stub
Server discovers existing DBs from dirs::data_dir()/seshat/repos/*.db
If no scanned projects (no *.db files): error with suggestion to run 'seshat scan'
Single-repo mode: if multiple DBs exist, use most recently modified; log which DB is loaded
Startup displays: version, loaded repo info (name, branch, conventions count, files count), 'Watcher: not available', transport info
Ctrl+C triggers graceful shutdown: drain active requests (5s timeout), close DB, display uptime
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DB path resolution may reuse existing logic from scan.rs. Watcher line always shows 'not available' (deferred to Epic 9). Config loading: seshat-cli loads AppConfig, extracts ServerConfig, passes to McpServer.

userStories.title: Response envelope and error handling structs
userStories.description: As an AI agent developer, I want consistent JSON response envelopes for all MCP tools so I can parse any tool response with one schema.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/seshat-mcp/src/envelope.rs implements ResponseEnvelope<T> with fields: status, tool, repo, branch, scope, duration_ms, data, metadata
envelope.rs implements ErrorEnvelope with fields: status='error', tool, repo, error: {code, message, suggestion}
metadata struct includes next_steps: Vec<String> for context-aware hints
scope field always returns 'root' (multi-repo scoping deferred)
Error codes defined as enum: REPO_NOT_SCANNED, EMPTY_TOPIC, INVALID_INPUT, NODE_NOT_FOUND, NOT_USER_DECISION, INTERNAL_ERROR
Duration measured via Instant::now() -> elapsed() pattern
Code snippets in responses truncated at 20 lines with 'truncated: true' flag
All envelope types implement Serialize + Send + Sync
Unit tests verify serialization of success and error envelopes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Input validation runs before graph logic; invalid input returns structured error. Every tool call should be logged via tracing with tool name, duration, result status.

userStories.title: Persist convention detector results to nodes table after scan
userStories.description: As a developer, I need convention detector results persisted to the database so the MCP server can query them.
acceptanceCriteria: After aggregate_findings() in scan.rs, conventions are persisted to the nodes table as KnowledgeNode records
Convention nodes use: nature = finding's nature, weight = finding's weight, confidence = finding's confidence, adoption_count/total_count from aggregation, description = convention description
ext_data JSON stores: detector_name, trend, evidence[] (with file, line, end_line, snippet), source: 'auto_detected'
crates/seshat-storage gets new repository method: find_conventions_by_branch(branch_id) -> Vec<KnowledgeNode>
Re-scan: old auto-detected convention nodes are replaced (DELETE where source = 'auto_detected' + re-insert)
User-recorded decisions (source = 'user') are NEVER deleted or overwritten during re-scan
Unit tests: persist conventions, verify re-scan replaces auto-detected but preserves user decisions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL PATH story. Currently AggregatedConvention results are computed in-memory during scan and never written to DB. This story fixes that. Convention persistence happens after aggregate_findings() and before build_report_data().

userStories.title: FTS5 migration and full-text search index management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an FTS5 full-text search index on convention descriptions so that MCP tools can search conventions by topic.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration V4__add_conventions_fts.sql creates standalone FTS5 virtual table: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE conventions_fts USING fts5(description, node_id, detector_name)
Migration V4 also adds convention_compliance_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 to files_ir table
crates/seshat-graph/src/fts.rs implements rebuild_fts_index() — DELETE all rows, re-INSERT from convention nodes in nodes table
fts.rs implements search_conventions(query: &str) -> Vec<NodeId> using FTS5 MATCH query
FTS5 index rebuilt after every scan (called from scan.rs after convention persistence)
Unit tests: populate conventions, rebuild FTS5 index, search by keywords, verify relevant results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses standalone FTS5 table (not external content table) to avoid sync complexity. The table has its own rowid and stores description + node_id for joining back.

userStories.title: Golden files computation and per-file convention compliance
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I need golden files (top convention-compliant files) identified so I can reference them as exemplars.
acceptanceCriteria: During convention persistence (after aggregate_findings), per-file convention compliance count is computed from follows_convention=true findings
convention_compliance_count updated in files_ir table for each file
crates/seshat-graph/src/golden_files.rs implements get_golden_files(limit: usize) returning top files by convention_compliance_count
Golden files include: path, conventions_count, last_modified (from last_commit_date)
Default limit is 5 files
Unit tests: populate files_ir with compliance counts, query golden files, verify ordering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: convention_compliance_count column added in US-006 migration. This story computes and writes the values, and provides the query function.

userStories.title: query_project_context MCP tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to query project context so I understand the project's stack and structure before generating code.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tool query_project_context registered with rmcp (replace dummy ping tool)
crates/seshat-graph/src/project_context.rs implements all data aggregation logic
crates/seshat-mcp/src/tools/project_context.rs is thin: parse input -> call graph -> wrap in envelope
Response data contains: languages[], modules[], dependencies (with canonical per domain), conventions_count, confidence_summary
data.golden_files[]: top 5 files from golden_files module
confidence_summary: {high_count, medium_count, low_count, high_ratio} where high = confidence > 85%
Optional focus_area parameter filters results to a specific domain
data.submodules[] present but empty array
metadata.next_steps suggests querying specific conventions based on detected patterns
Response time < 1 second
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Languages: query files_ir grouped by language. Dependencies: re-run discover_manifests() on serve startup (simplest approach, manifest files are small). Modules: query nodes table for module-type nodes. seshat-mcp has NO direct storage access — all queries go through seshat-graph.

userStories.title: query_convention MCP tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to query conventions for a topic so I know how things are done before generating code.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tool query_convention registered with rmcp
topic parameter (required) searched via FTS5 against convention descriptions

Response data.conventions[] contains: id, nature, weight, confidence, adoption (count, total, rate), trend, description, source (auto_detected/user), user_confirmed, examples[] with {file, line, end_line, snippet, truncated}
Returns both auto-detected conventions AND user-recorded decisions matching the topic
Removed decisions (ext_data.removed = true) are filtered out
Empty result = success with empty conventions array (not an error)
Empty topic parameter = error EMPTY_TOPIC with suggestion
crates/seshat-graph/src/conventions.rs implements query_convention() with FTS5 search + data enrichment
metadata includes query, results_count, search_type: 'fts5'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: FTS5 query: SELECT node_id, rank FROM conventions_fts WHERE conventions_fts MATCH ? ORDER BY rank. Then load full KnowledgeNode by IDs, extract ext_data for evidence/trend/source.

userStories.title: record_decision MCP tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to record conventions and decisions that automated detectors cannot discover, so that project-specific rules are captured.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tool record_decision registered with rmcp
Parameters: description (required), nature (Decision/Convention/Rule, default Decision), weight (Rule/Strong, default Strong), category (optional), examples[] (optional), reason (optional)
Creates KnowledgeNode with ext_data.source = 'user', ext_data.user_confirmed = true
Node is immediately active — visible in query_convention results
Node is NEVER overwritten or deleted by automated re-scanning (guarded by source check)
FTS5 index updated with new node's description (INSERT into conventions_fts)
Response confirms creation with node ID
Input validation: description required and non-empty, nature must be valid enum value, weight must be valid
crates/seshat-graph/src/decisions.rs implements record logic
metadata.next_steps suggests: 'Use query_convention to verify this decision appears in results'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stored in existing nodes table. Distinguished by ext_data.source = 'user'. category maps to ext_data grouping for filtering.

userStories.title: update_decision and remove_decision MCP tools
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to update or remove previously recorded decisions so the knowledge graph stays current.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tools update_decision and remove_decision registered with rmcp
update_decision parameters: id (required), plus any fields to change (description, nature, weight, category, examples, reason)
remove_decision parameters: id (required), reason (required)
Only user-recorded decisions (ext_data.source = 'user') can be modified; auto-detected returns error NOT_USER_DECISION with suggestion
update_decision: updates node fields + ext_data, re-indexes FTS5 (DELETE old row + INSERT new)
remove_decision: soft-deletes (sets ext_data.removed = true, removed_reason, removed_at = timestamp)
Removed decisions no longer appear in query_convention results
Response confirms update/removal with node ID
crates/seshat-graph/src/decisions.rs implements update/remove logic
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Soft-delete preserves audit trail. All graph queries must filter out ext_data.removed = true. FTS5: DELETE only for removals.

userStories.title: Agent protocol documentation in MCP tool descriptions
userStories.description: As an AI agent developer, I want clear tool descriptions so agents follow the understand-work-update loop correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Each tool's description in rmcp registration includes: purpose, when to use, parameter guidance
Protocol documented: (1) Query conventions before work -> (2) Do work -> (3) If new convention discovered, suggest recording it
Common scenarios covered: wrapper conventions, architectural decisions, team style agreements
Tool descriptions are concise but sufficient for AI agent self-guidance (< 500 chars each)
All 5 tools (query_project_context, query_convention, record_decision, update_decision, remove_decision) have descriptions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: rmcp's tool registration allows setting description per tool — this is what AI agents see in list_tools. Can be done in parallel with other tool stories, but placed last since it needs all tools to exist.
